name: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy (Async)
description: Python FastAPI with async SQLAlchemy 2.x, Pydantic v2, Alembic, and PostgreSQL
conventions: Use async/await everywhere — all route handlers, database calls, and service functions must be async
Pydantic v2 schemas for all request and response models — always separate from SQLAlchemy models
SQLAlchemy models in app/models/ — Pydantic schemas in app/schemas/ — never mix them
Use Alembic for all database migrations — never modify the DB schema manually
Dependency injection via FastAPI's Depends() — use it for DB sessions, auth, and shared services
Organize routers by domain in app/routers/ — one file per resource (users.py, orders.py, etc.)
Use HTTPException with appropriate status codes — never return error dicts with 200 status
All database sessions via async context manager — never leave sessions open across requests
Strict type hints everywhere — use `from __future__ import annotations` at the top of every file
Test with pytest-asyncio — mark async tests with @pytest.mark.asyncio
Background tasks via FastAPI BackgroundTasks or Celery — never block the event loop
Use pydantic-settings for config — never read os.environ directly in business logic
Return 404 via HTTPException when a resource is not found — check before any update/delete
Use UUID primary keys for all models — never integer auto-increment for public APIs
